tasks.title: Fix run-loop stop signal handling (sequential + parallel)
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs:219-233 only checks stop signal on successful task completion; failures skip stop handling.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:263-282 logs stop signal but never clears it.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:446-454 does not exit when stop signal is set and no workers are in flight (next task exists => loop continues).
crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/stop.rs:7-8 notes signal cleanup is handled by the run loop after detection.
plan: In sequential run_loop, check for stop signal after any task attempt (success or failure), clear it, and exit cleanly.
In parallel run_loop, if stop signal is set and there are no in-flight workers, break the loop and clear the signal; avoid the idle spin when tasks remain but stop is requested.
Add regression coverage for stop-signal behavior in both sequential and parallel paths (including failure path and idle stop), and update docs if behavior changes.

tasks.title: Honor git_commit_push_enabled overrides in parallel workers
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/worker.rs:139-150 unconditionally adds --git-commit-push-on to worker args.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/args.rs:18-91 does not map git_commit_push_enabled overrides into CLI args, so --git-commit-push-off is ignored in parallel mode.
plan: Add git_commit_push_enabled mapping to build_override_args and remove/conditionalize the hardcoded --git-commit-push-on in worker command construction.
Decide whether auto_pr/auto_merge should force commit/push and document the behavior explicitly if it remains required.
Add tests for parallel worker arg construction with git_commit_push_on/off overrides and update CLI docs to reflect the final behavior.

tasks.title: Align docs with parallel.workers default behavior
evidence: docs/configuration.md:220-222 claims parallel is disabled unless CLI --parallel is used.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs:90-94 enables parallel whenever config.parallel.workers is set (even without --parallel).
plan: Decide whether parallel.workers should implicitly enable parallel runs without --parallel.
If code is correct, update docs/CLI help to state that setting parallel.workers enables parallel by default; otherwise, change run_loop to require --parallel and add a test for the intended behavior.
Verify help text and configuration docs are consistent across docs/configuration.md and docs/cli.md.

tasks.title: Make parallel loop avoid queue.json merge conflicts without losing task state
evidence: CLI: `ralph run loop --parallel <N>` enables parallel mode (clap wiring in `crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs`, dispatch in `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs`).
Parallel mode clones workspaces and copies `.ralph/queue.json` and `.ralph/done.json` into each workspace (`sync_ralph_state` in `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/sync.rs`).

Parallel workers run `ralph run one --parallel-worker` in the workspace; `mark_task_doing` writes `.ralph/queue.json` via `queue::save_queue` (`crates/ralph/src/commands/run/context.rs`, `crates/ralph/src/queue.rs`).

Merge runner attempts to merge PRs and can invoke AI conflict resolution (`crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs`), so worker-branch edits to `.ralph/queue.json`/`.ralph/done.json` can repeatedly surface as Git merge conflicts.

Parallel state persists in `.ralph/cache/parallel/state.json` (`crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/state.rs`) and the supervisor holds a queue lock for selection, but Git-level conflicts still occur when multiple branches modify the same queue/done files.
plan: Reproduce on a clean branch: run `ralph run loop --parallel 2 --max-tasks 2`, then capture which PR/merge step produces conflicts on `.ralph/queue.json` and/or `.ralph/done.json` (include `git status` + conflict file list).

Inventory all queue/done mutation sites that can run inside a parallel worker (e.g., `mark_task_doing`, task completion transitions, queue save/archive helpers) and map them to the parallel workflow (workspace sync → worker run → PR creation → merge).

Define the desired parallel invariant: only the coordinator (base branch) mutates `.ralph/queue.json`/`.ralph/done.json`; workers emit a minimal, deterministic per-task "delta" (status transitions + timestamps + any task field updates) for the coordinator to apply under the queue lock.

Implement coordinator-applied deltas in `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs` (or adjacent module) and ensure worker branches do not include tracked changes to `.ralph/queue.json`/`.ralph/done.json` (adjust `sync_ralph_state`/worker setup accordingly).

Add tests that simulate multiple workers completing in close succession and assert: no queue/done diffs in worker branches, no dropped tasks, no queue wipes, completed tasks reliably move to done, and reruns are idempotent.

Update `docs/cli.md` and `ralph run loop --help` to document parallel queue semantics (explicitly: coordinator-managed queue/done, how completion is applied, and any behavior changes vs non-parallel).
Run `make ci` and `ralph queue validate`, then do a manual merge of two worker branches to confirm `.ralph/queue.json`/`.ralph/done.json` are conflict-free.
notes: `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/loop/` no longer exists; run loop dispatch (including parallel `--parallel`) lives in `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs` and clap wiring is in `crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs`.

Parallel state is persisted at `.ralph/cache/parallel/state.json`; the current parallel workflow copies queue/done into each workspace and worker runs write to queue, which aligns with the reported persistent merge conflicts.
Force-add parallel worker completion signals so ignored cache paths don't drop status/notes.
Added git helper for forced staging and exercised it in supervision tests.
CI: make ci; ralph queue validate.
tasks.request: When using 'ralph run loop --parallel 2' there are ALWAYS merge conflicts due to the queue.json changes. We need to figure out a way to handle this to avoid merge conflicts. We need to make sure we don't wipe out the queue and we properly mark tasks complete and move the tasks to the done.json file too. We need to be able to reliably merge and mimic the currently reliable workflow of 'ralph run loop' when it runs without parallel workers.

tasks.title: Fix parallel state.json to auto-correct and auto-populate git branch
evidence: User request: parallel run previously wrote `.ralph/cache/parallel/state.json` with base_branch='02022026'; later running on base_branch='main' fails unless the user manually clears the state file.

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs bails early when `existing.base_branch != current_branch` with: "Parallel state base branch '{}' does not match current branch '{}'" (no auto-correction path).
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs checks base_branch mismatch before pruning stale `tasks_in_flight`, so a stale state file can block cleanup/recovery.

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/state.rs hardcodes the state path to `.ralph/cache/parallel/state.json` and uses `serde(deny_unknown_fields)` + JSONC parsing, so legacy/unknown fields will fail deserialization.
crates/ralph/src/git/branch.rs resolves current branch via `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` and errors on detached HEAD, so parallel runs require a named branch.
Current repo state: `.ralph/cache/parallel/state.json` exists and currently has base_branch='main' with empty `tasks_in_flight`/`prs`, which is a safe shape for an auto-heal rewrite on mismatch.
plan: Read `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs` state bootstrap flow (load/save, base_branch validation, stale task pruning) and map the exact failure paths.

Define conservative auto-heal rules for base_branch mismatch (e.g., after pruning stale tasks and reconciling PR records, allow rewrite only when there are no in-flight tasks and no blocking/open PR records).

Implement base_branch auto-correction in the parallel startup path: if mismatch and state is safe-to-reset, rewrite state.base_branch to the current branch and persist; otherwise fail with an actionable error that references the state file path and recovery options.
Add unit tests that cover: mismatch + empty state auto-heals; mismatch + active tasks/prs stays a hard error; mismatch + stale PID/workspace is pruned then auto-heals.
Update `docs/cli.md` and `docs/workflow.md` to document the new behavior and the expected recovery workflow for parallel state files.
Run `make ci`.
notes: The current mismatch check runs before stale `tasks_in_flight` pruning; moving or augmenting this logic is likely required so crash-recovery can self-heal instead of hard-failing.
Auto-correction should avoid rewriting state when it could belong to an active parallel run (in-flight tasks or open PRs).
Guard missing base_branch by requiring safe state before auto-populating or retargeting.
Added tests for missing base_branch auto-heal and unsafe in-flight cases; kept mismatch coverage.
make ci passes.
tasks.request: Fix the state.json file to auto-correct and auto-populate and set the git branch properly. User previously ran a parallel run on branch '02022026' but has since changed to 'main' and this fails unless user manually clears .ralph/cache/parallel/state.json.

tasks.title: Prevent parallel loop from re-running tasks when no PR record exists
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/worker.rs:71-90 excludes in-flight tasks and only PRs with lifecycle==open && merged==false; tasks with no PR record are not excluded and can be selected again.

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:326-417 removes finished tasks from tasks_in_flight regardless of whether a PR record was created; PR records are only upserted inside the settings.auto_pr branches and PR creation failures only log warnings.

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:95-108 automatically disables auto_pr/auto_merge/draft_on_failure when git commit/push is disabled, making no-PR parallel runs possible even if config defaults enable PR automation.

docs/cli.md:581-591 states parallel workers do not modify .ralph/queue.json/.ralph/done.json and completion signals are applied only after a PR merge; if PR automation is skipped, the coordinator queue state stays unchanged and the same todo task remains runnable.
docs/configuration.md:216-240 documents that parallel.auto_pr can be set false (and is auto-disabled when commit/push is disabled), which currently allows repeated selection in parallel mode.

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/state.rs:11-14 and 24-37 define .ralph/cache/parallel/state.json with tasks_in_flight and prs only; there is no durable marker for finished tasks that have no PR record.
plan: Reproduce: run `ralph run loop --parallel 2 --max-tasks 2` with `parallel.auto_pr=false` (or `--git-commit-push-off`) and confirm the same task ID is selected again after completion.

Decide and document the intended policy for parallel runs when PR automation is disabled or PR creation fails (recommended: treat finished tasks as blocked/pending PR and prevent re-selection until user action).

Implement the policy: either hard-error when auto_pr is effectively disabled in parallel mode, or persist a durable needs_pr/completed_without_pr marker in .ralph/cache/parallel/state.json and include it in the exclusion set during selection.
Add tests for auto_pr=false, git-commit-push-off, and PR creation failure; assert tasks are not re-selected indefinitely and the state persists across restarts.
Update docs/configuration.md and docs/cli.md to describe the new behavior/constraints and any recovery steps for clearing blocked state.
notes: Today, selection can loop forever when a task finishes but no PR record exists (auto_pr disabled or gh PR creation failure), because queue/done remain unchanged and only open PRs are excluded.

Parallel already reconciles PR records on startup, but reconciliation does not update queue/done; consider whether merged PRs should remain blocking until the coordinator observes and applies completion updates.
Add finished-without-PR blockers to prevent parallel re-runs without PRs
Clarify recovery guidance for no-PR blockers in docs and logs
make ci

tasks.title: Ensure workspace cleanup when parallel worker spawn fails
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:267-283 creates/syncs a workspace then calls spawn_worker(..)? before guard.register_workspace(..); if spawn_worker fails, the function returns early and the workspace directory is left on disk.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:298-299 registers the workspace only after a successful spawn_worker, so failure paths never reach workspace registration.

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/cleanup_guard.rs:188-199 removes only workspaces tracked in ParallelCleanupGuard.workspaces (registered via register_workspace); it does not remove untracked workspace directories.
plan: In crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs, register the workspace with ParallelCleanupGuard immediately after git::create_workspace_at (and sync_ralph_state), before calling spawn_worker.
Confirm workspace cleanup remains safe and idempotent when a workspace is pre-registered (manual removals should continue to be no-ops due to path.exists checks).

Add regression coverage that forces a spawn_worker failure and asserts the workspace directory is removed via guard drop/cleanup (likely by injecting a failing spawner or factoring the spawn step into a testable helper).
Run cargo test -p ralph and make ci to verify parallel cleanup behavior and prevent regressions.
notes: Scope updated: removed non-existent crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/tests.rs; current unit tests for parallel cleanup live in crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/cleanup_guard.rs under #[cfg(test)].
Current code still registers workspaces after spawn_worker succeeds, so a spawn_worker error can leak a workspace directory until manually cleaned.
Register workspaces before sync/spawn so cleanup handles early failures.
Added regression tests for sync and spawn failure cleanup paths.
CI: make ci

tasks.title: Stop defaulting missing parallel completion signals to Done
evidence: Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:448-458 (ensure_completion_signal_in_workspace writes a completion signal with status TaskStatus::Done when missing)

Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:383-385 (apply_completion_and_collect_bytes calls ensure_completion_signal_in_workspace then apply_phase3_completion_signal, which can finalize the task)
Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/phase3.rs:298-347 (apply_phase3_completion_signal completes the task from the completion signal, then removes the signal)
Path: crates/ralph/src/completions.rs:85-93 (completion signals only allow terminal statuses done/rejected; there is no "unknown" status)
Test: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:970-1037 covers the happy-path (signal present); missing-signal behavior is currently untested
Repro: remove .ralph/cache/completions/<TASK_ID>.json on the base branch, then run parallel merge; merge runner synthesizes a Done completion signal and finalizes the task
plan: Change ensure_completion_signal_in_workspace to return an error when the completion signal is missing (do not synthesize TaskStatus::Done).
Update merge runner flow to surface the error and avoid mutating queue/done when the completion signal is missing.
Add a unit test in merge_runner.rs covering missing completion signal: expect an error and verify no queue/done mutations are applied.
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph`.
notes: Current merge runner behavior can incorrectly mark tasks Done during parallel merges when no completion signal exists; erroring is safer than guessing.
Completion signals only support done/rejected; representing an "unknown" status would be a larger contract change, so missing signals should be treated as a hard error here.
Stop treating missing parallel completion signals as Done; now errors if absent in base-sync.
Updated merge-runner tests and CLI/docs to reflect missing-signal error path.
CI: make ci

tasks.title: Validate queue/done bytes before applying merge results
evidence: Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:890-925 (apply_merge_queue_sync persists queue/done/productivity bytes directly; bypasses queue parsing + semantic validation)

Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:423-452 (apply_completion_and_collect_bytes reads queue/done bytes from the base-sync workspace and returns them without validating as QueueFile JSONC + semantics)
Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:270-342 (validate_queue_done_in_workspace exists but is only invoked after AI conflict resolution, not for clean merges)
Risk/Repro: if a merged PR introduces unparseable or semantically invalid queue/done contents, the parallel merge flow can write those bytes into the local repo, breaking subsequent queue operations
plan: In apply_merge_queue_sync, parse queue_bytes and optional done_bytes into QueueFile values (use the same JSONC parsing rules as queue::load_queue) and validate them via queue::validate_queue/validate_queue_set before touching disk.
Serialize validated QueueFile values and atomically write via queue::save_queue (and remove done_path when done_bytes is None); avoid writing raw bytes from MergeResult.
On any parse/validation error, return an error and leave local queue/done/productivity untouched; include task_id and the failing context in the error chain.
Add unit tests for apply_merge_queue_sync: rejects invalid queue bytes, rejects invalid done bytes, accepts missing done bytes, and preserves existing files on failure.
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph`.
notes: merge_runner validates queue/done in the merge workspace only after AI conflict resolution, but the clean-merge path still produces bytes that are applied locally without any parse/validation gate.
queue parsing supports JSONC (comments + trailing commas) and semantic validation helpers exist, but apply_merge_queue_sync currently bypasses them entirely.
Validate merge queue/done bytes with JSONC parsing + semantic checks before write
Persist normalized queue/done via save_queue; skip writes on validation failure
Added apply_merge_queue_sync tests for parse/UTF-8/semantic failures and JSONC success
make ci

tasks.title: Reject PR heads that do not match the parallel branch prefix
evidence: Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:119-126 (task_id derived from PR head + configured branch_prefix)
Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:605-612 (task_id_from_branch strips prefix but falls back to returning the raw head when prefix mismatches)
Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:230 (workspace_root.join(task_id) treats task_id as a path segment; a task_id with '/' creates nested paths)
Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:634-637 (unit test asserts prefix mismatch returns 'feature/RQ-0002', codifying the problematic behavior)

Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/state.rs:205-225 (ParallelPrRecord::pr_info prefers stored head; if branch_prefix changes, stored head may no longer match and merge runner currently derives an invalid task_id)
plan: Introduce a shared helper to parse task_id from PR head that requires prefix match, strips it, and validates the remaining task ID against resolved id_prefix/id_width (and rejects '/' or '..').
Update merge_runner to use the helper; on invalid/mismatched heads, log a warning with PR number/head and skip merging that PR without terminating the merge thread.
Persist a human-readable blocker/warning in the parallel state file so resume runs surface the mismatch and operators can fix config/branches intentionally.
Update tests: replace the current fallback behavior test with mismatch-is-error, and add coverage for invalid IDs (whitespace, wrong width/prefix, path separators).
Verify via `cargo test -p ralph`, then run `make ci`.
notes: merge_runner.rs module docs assume PR heads use the configured prefix, but task_id_from_branch currently accepts mismatches by returning the raw head.
Because task_id is used in filesystem paths (workspace roots and completion signal locations), allowing arbitrary head strings risks nested paths and broken queue/done lookups.
Validate parallel PR heads against branch_prefix and carry trusted task_id into merge runner.
Persist merge_blocker in parallel state and skip blocked PRs.
Added validation/blocker tests and documented branch_prefix head requirements.

tasks.title: Enforce clean-repo preflight for parallel run loop
evidence: Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:72-125 (run_loop_parallel lacks clean-repo enforcement - no require_clean_repo_ignoring_paths call)
Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs:604-613 (run_one_impl enforces clean repo via require_clean_repo_ignoring_paths with RALPH_RUN_CLEAN_ALLOWED_PATHS)
Path: crates/ralph/src/git/clean.rs:25-30 (RALPH_RUN_CLEAN_ALLOWED_PATHS defines allowed dirty paths)
Repro: create uncommitted change outside allowed paths, then run `ralph run loop --parallel 2`; it proceeds without the clean-repo guard
plan: Add a preflight clean-repo check to run_loop_parallel using git::require_clean_repo_ignoring_paths with RALPH_RUN_CLEAN_ALLOWED_PATHS (same as run_one_impl).
Honor --force to bypass the check, consistent with non-parallel behavior.
Add a unit/integration test that parallel run rejects dirty repos when --force is not set.
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph` and confirming the new test passes.
notes: Scope expanded to include git/clean.rs for RALPH_RUN_CLEAN_ALLOWED_PATHS reference and gitutil_test.rs for test patterns
Added clean-repo preflight check to run_loop_parallel
Uses git::require_clean_repo_ignoring_paths with RALPH_RUN_CLEAN_ALLOWED_PATHS
Honors --force flag to bypass check (consistent with run_one_impl)
Added unit test run_loop_parallel_rejects_dirty_repo_without_force
make ci passes (1747+ tests)

tasks.title: Count open PRs toward parallel max_tasks on resume
evidence: Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:1133-1139 (initial_tasks_started counts tasks_in_flight and finished_without_pr, but ignores open PRs)
Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/worker.rs:83-88 (open PRs are excluded from selection, but not counted toward max_tasks)
Repro: create state with an open PR record, run with --max-tasks 1; a new task still starts
plan: Include open (unmerged) PR records in initial_tasks_started so max_tasks reflects work already completed in a previous parallel run.
Add a unit test that builds a state file with open PRs and asserts max_tasks prevents over-starting on resume.
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph` and confirming the new test passes.
notes: Fixed: open/unmerged PRs now count toward max_tasks on resume
Added is_open_unmerged() helper to ParallelPrRecord
Updated initial_tasks_started() to include open/unmerged PRs
Refactored 4 existing locations to use shared helper
Added unit test: resume_open_prs_count_toward_max_tasks

tasks.title: Avoid permanent blockers from finished_without_pr records
evidence: Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:961-998 (record_finished_without_pr writes a blocker entry on PR creation failure or auto_pr disabled)
Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/worker.rs:89-91 (finished_without_pr entries are always excluded from selection via collect_excluded_ids)
Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/state.rs:246-279 (ParallelFinishedWithoutPrRecord struct with reason field - AutoPrDisabled, DraftPrDisabled, PrCreateFailed, etc.)
Repro: run parallel with auto_pr disabled; successful tasks are recorded as finished_without_pr and are blocked from rerun without manual state edits
plan: Define an expiration/rehydration policy for finished_without_pr records (e.g., TTL or allow rerun when reason is AutoPrDisabled/DraftPrDisabled).
Update collect_excluded_ids in worker.rs and state pruning to honor the policy.
Add tests covering both transient PR failures and auto_pr disabled scenarios.
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph` and confirming the new tests pass.
notes: Implemented TTL-based expiration and rehydration policy for finished_without_pr records
AutoPrDisabled/DraftPrDisabled records now rehydrate when settings change
PrCreateFailed records expire after 24h TTL (matches session timeout)
Added comprehensive tests for TTL, rehydration, and workspace existence checks
Updated existing tests to work with new conditional blocking behavior

tasks.title: Prune in-flight tasks with missing PID using a TTL
evidence: Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:927-954 (prune_stale_tasks_in_flight retains records when pid is None)
Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/state.rs:65-92 (tasks_in_flight stored in state file and used for exclusions)
Repro: create state.json with a tasks_in_flight entry that has pid=null and an existing workspace path; parallel run will never drop it and capacity stays blocked
plan: Extend ParallelTaskRecord with started_at (or last_seen) and persist it when spawning workers.
Update prune_stale_tasks_in_flight to drop pid-less records older than a safe TTL and log a warning.
Add tests for pid-missing records both within and beyond the TTL.
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph` and confirming the new tests pass.
notes: Extended ParallelTaskRecord with started_at field (with backward compat via serde(default))
Updated prune_stale_tasks_in_flight to TTL-prune pid-less records older than 24h
Added 3 new tests for TTL pruning scenarios (within TTL, beyond TTL, missing started_at)
All tests pass including 6 prune-specific tests and full CI gate
No related tasks require updates

tasks.title: Add preflight queue/done validation in parallel loop
evidence: Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:72-140 (run_loop_parallel has no queue validation step before spawning workers)
Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/worker.rs:45-70 (select_next_task_locked loads queue but does not validate)
Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs:460-475 (run_one_impl validates queue/done before execution using load_queue_with_repair_and_validate)
Path: crates/ralph/src/queue.rs:113 (load_queue_with_repair_and_validate function available for use)

plan: Add queue/done validation at the start of run_loop_parallel using load_queue_with_repair_and_validate (similar to run_one_impl).
Abort the parallel run with a clear error if validation fails (fail fast before spawning workers).
Add a test that invalid queue.json fails early in parallel mode (before any workers are spawned).
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph` and confirming the new test passes.
notes: Validation should happen after queue lock acquisition but before worker spawning.
Consider logging any validation warnings similar to run_one_impl behavior.
Added preflight queue/done validation in run_loop_parallel() with fail-fast behavior
Fixed Ctrl-C handler test conflict for sequential test execution
Added 2 unit tests verifying early validation failure before worker spawning

tasks.title: Fail fast if gh CLI is missing when auto PR/merge is enabled
evidence: Path: crates/ralph/src/git/pr.rs:1-14 (module docs state 'gh is installed and authenticated' but no runtime check)
Path: crates/ralph/src/git/pr.rs:95-118 (create_pr calls gh directly without availability check)
Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:72-163 (run_loop_parallel has preflight checks for queue but not for gh)

tasks.title: Clean up merge-runner base-sync workspace after use
evidence: Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:409 (apply_completion_and_collect_bytes creates workspace_root/.base-sync)

Current behavior: base-sync workspace is created at line 409 but never removed after merge completion

tasks.title: Fix parallel gh preflight test to assert correct state file path
evidence: code: crates/ralph/tests/parallel_gh_preflight_test.rs:104 uses .ralph/cache/parallel_state.json (WRONG)
